name: Quebec-Style Roast Goose
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the stuffing by combining bread, currants, apples, thyme, salt, pepper, and melted butter.
2. Stuff the goose with the stuffing, then truss and tie it.
3. Prick the goose all over with a fork.
4. Heat oil in a roasting pan on top of the stove. Brown the goose lightly on all sides, then drain off any pan drippings.
5. Set the goose breast side up, add a little water, and cover. Roast at 375°F for one hour.
6. Combine chopped onion, carrot, celery, garlic, bay leaf, cloves, thyme, and marjoram. Discard any fat from the roasting pan, then add the vegetable mixture and continue roasting uncovered for 20-25 minutes per pound (total of three to four hours), draining off fat at intervals as needed.
7. Transfer the cooked goose to a platter and keep warm. Skim off remaining fat in the pan, then heat the drippings and vegetables on top of the stove until the mixture is reduced.
8. Stir in white wine, tomato paste, and chicken bouillon. Simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the gravy. If desired, thicken the gravy by blending a little cornstarch with water.
9. Serve the goose with gravy, applesauce, mashed potatoes, and braised cabbage. Makes six to eight servings.
